Explanation: Using selectFrom(CUSTOMERS) fetches all columns from the CUSTOMERS table, which may retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider using select() with only the specific columns needed, such as ctx.select(CUSTOMERS.FIRSTNAME, CUSTOMERS.LASTNAME).from(CUSTOMERS).fetch().

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from both CUSTOMERS and INVOICES tables, which retrieves unnecessary data. Explicitly list only the required columns, e.g., jooq.select(CUSTOMERS.CUSTOMERID, CUSTOMERS.FIRSTNAME, INVOICES.INVOICEID).from(...).

Explanation: The NAME column is marked as NULLABLE (no `.nullable(false)` constraint) but likely should never be NULL for an artist record, as an artist without a name has no practical meaning. Consider adding a NOT NULL constraint to this column to enforce data integrity.
